gaps.gap: No risks section: failure modes appear only as counter-metrics, with no mitigations or named owners.
gaps.suggestion: Add a lean risks table (risk + mitigation + owner) and an assumptions list (validated vs unvalidated).

gaps.gap: Problem stated qualitatively with no baseline numbers.
gaps.suggestion: Add one baseline figure, e.g. follow-ups lost across the last N /implement runs.

gaps.gap: Personas are role categories, not a named persona.
gaps.suggestion: Name P1 concretely (e.g. 'Ashwini, Shield maintainer running /implement daily').

gaps.gap: Three of four metrics use vague targets ('Majority', 'often enough', 'one step').
gaps.suggestion: Attach numeric thresholds and a time horizon, e.g. '>=70% reach a terminal state within 30 days'.

gaps.gap: No tracking owner, tool, or cadence named for the metrics.
gaps.suggestion: Name how metrics are measured (periodic /backlog audit, or git history of backlog.json).

gaps.gap: Why-now describes a standing capability gap, not a concrete trigger.
gaps.suggestion: Anchor urgency to a recent specific instance of lost follow-up work.
Quantify (items dropped per implement cycle, or hours re-deriving scope).
Mark each assumption validated (with evidence) or unvalidated; the no-hooks bet is the key unvalidated one.

gaps.gap: Flows are happy-path only — no error/abandon/missing-plan paths.
gaps.suggestion: Add >=1 named failure/recovery path per core flow (missing plan.json, abandoned capture, concurrent write).

gaps.gap: With ids removed, the PRD never specifies how a proposed-new epic name is matched to the eventual real epic in plan.json.
gaps.suggestion: Specify the match key (string match vs user-confirmed at promotion) — this is the removal-correctness heart.

gaps.gap: M3 states '/backlog reconciles on view' as settled, but §9 still lists the reconciliation trigger as Open.
gaps.suggestion: Resolve the §9 open question or soften M3 to match.

gaps.gap: kind field is committed in §6/M1 ('schema defined') but left open in §9.
gaps.suggestion: Decide the kind field before M1 can be built.

gaps.gap: Capture 'usable from any Shield skill' but no command/helper/write-contract specified.
gaps.suggestion: Define the capture entrypoint (slash command, helper, or write contract).

gaps.gap: Several §10 out-of-scope items are bare list entries with no why-deferred.
gaps.suggestion: Append a short why-deferred clause to each rationale-less §10 item.

gaps.gap: No scope-creep guard naming the likely creep ask + decision authority.
gaps.suggestion: Add a one-line guard naming the probable creep ask and that @ashwinimanoj gates expansion.
'N/A — internal tooling, no Legal/Security/Support surface (confirmed by @ashwinimanoj)'.

gaps.gap: §6 says entries 'removed automatically' but reconciliation runs on /backlog view (user action); §6 non-goal disclaims automatic machinery.
gaps.suggestion: Replace 'removed automatically' with 'removed on next /backlog view'.

informational_nfr_notes: Atomic write + concurrency for backlog.json (write-temp-then-rename; capture vs reconcile-rewrite race).
Add schema_version so open shape decisions (ordering, kind) evolve via read-old/write-new.
Reconciliation should no-op (never remove) on missing/old manifest.json or plan.json, not error.
backlog.json git-tracked → bad reconciliation is git-revertable; consider dry-run/confirm before removals.
